title: MLPerf Inference Benchmark
claim: Prescribes rules/best practices for comparability across architectures; first round: 600+ reproducible measurements, 14 orgs, 30+ systems; scenario detail taken from the living rules doc, not this abstract

title: MLCommons MLPerf Inference Rules (inference_rules.adoc)
locator: scenarios table ('Scenario | Query Generation | Duration ...'), LoadGen operation section, and the Early Stopping Criterion appendix; commit c547732b539cb3a14cc5680597714c8c1df4cad0 (2026-07-07), re-verified 2026-07-11
claim: Four scenarios with distinct query-issue semantics: SingleStream and MultiStream are CLOSED-LOOP ('LoadGen sends next query as soon as SUT completes the previous query'; 90%-ile resp. 99%-ile early-stopping latency estimate); Server is OPEN-LOOP (queries per a scheduled Poisson distribution; max Poisson throughput under latency bound); Offline sends all samples at once (throughput). 600 s minimum duration in every scenario. The 23,886-query figure is the rules' worked example (90th pctile @99% confidence/0.5% margin) and the minimum-query table is 'a suggested starting point', NOT a universal required sample size; the early-stopping criterion permits fewer processed queries at the penalty of a higher effective percentile and applies only once the minimum run duration is met ('When the minimum run duration is met...') — it never replaces the 600 s duration. LLM TTFT/TPOT pairs (Llama3.1-8B 2000/100 conversational + 500/30 interactive; Llama2-70B 2000/200 + 450/40; Mixtral-8x7B 2000/200); caching of queries/responses/activation-derived values prohibited, KV-cache within batch allowed; smallest LLM workload Llama3.1-8B

title: Percentile at which MLPerf LLM TTFT/TPOT constraints bind
locator: per-benchmark constraint table not read in full this session
claim: Commonly stated as 99th percentile; NOT confirmed in the excerpts read — re-verify at P3-D-HW freeze

title: MLPerf Power: Benchmarking the Energy Efficiency of Machine Learning Systems from Microwatts to Megawatts
locator: methodology sections of the v2 HTML full text
claim: Full-system wall power as fundamental tenet; SPEC-certified analyzers (Yokogawa WT310) + PTDaemon; range-fixing calibration run then fixed analyzer ranges; >=60 s power data; energy by integrating samples over run window; Samples/Joule; datacenter boundary includes compute+interconnect, excludes cooling/storage nodes; 1,841 measurements across 60 systems; TDP is thermal limit not power proxy; cloud granularity pitfall

title: Beyond Chinchilla-Optimal: Accounting for Inference in Language Model Scaling Laws
locator: v2 HTML full text incl. worked examples and limitations
claim: 30B-quality @1e13 inference tokens -> 13.6B on 2.84x data, -28% total FLOPs; real-cost @1.5B requests -> 16B on 3.35T tokens, ~17% cost saving; 13B-quality @2T tokens -> 7B, -17% FLOPs; Chinchilla-70B @2T tokens: +1.3% FLOPs but 36% cost gap (inference ~1% MFU on output tokens vs ~50% training MFU); 47 models 150M-6B at 10-10,000 tokens/param; loss does not plateau at 10,000 t/p for 150M; Chinchilla fits on typical ratios overestimate extra-token value at extreme ratios. STATED LIMITS carried: the dollar model is explicitly simplified (fixed utilisation, no maintenance/refresh/demand-uncertainty); extreme-ratio scaling fits misbehave (authors refit); quality axis is pretraining loss, not a capability index — licenses the qualitative demand-changes-optimal-size proposition plus its own worked numbers under its own assumptions, nothing stronger

title: Part-time Power Measurements: nvidia-smi's Lack of Attention
claim: On A100 and H100 only ~25% of runtime is sampled for power; corrections vs external meters cut energy error 35% avg, up to 65%; 70+ GPUs across all generations since Fermi surveyed

title: RAPL in Action: Experiences in Using RAPL for Power Measurements
locator: abstract + Aalto research-portal summary (MAPE split from portal summary, not re-derived from paper body)
claim: RAPL highly correlated with plug power, negligible overhead; Sandy Bridge-EP systematic errors vs Haswell-EP near-perfect correlation (reported MAPE ~4.0% vs ~3.1% without DRAM domain — carried from portal summary); open issues: driver support, non-atomic register updates, unpredictable update timing; validated incl. EC2 replication

title: Reading RAPL energy measurements from Linux (V. Weaver reference page)
claim: 32-bit energy counters can wrap in ~60 s at high power (energy unit ~15.26 uJ) — poll inside wrap period; socket granularity only; unprivileged powercap sysfs reads restricted in Linux 5.10 (security), AMD no-permission-check RAPL driver removed in 5.13; perf interface needs root/paranoid setting

title: Local dev box exposes no RAPL (direct measurement)
claim: 2-vCPU Xeon Platinum 8259CL virtualized EC2, 7 GiB RAM, 50 GB NVMe EBS, no GPU; /sys/class/powercap absent -> CPU energy unmeasurable by counter on THIS host. The measured claim is scoped to this host only; extension to other virtualized/cloud hosts (Modal containers etc.) is a presumption assigned to the P3-D-HW per-host survey, not part of this measurement

title: NVML nvmlDeviceGetTotalEnergyConsumption — units, reset semantics, architecture floor
venue: NVIDIA NVML API reference + NVIDIA canonical nvml.h (go-nvml mirror, NVIDIA-authored)
locator: docstring above the nvmlDeviceGetTotalEnergyConsumption declaration (~line 6602); existence/signature also on https://docs.nvidia.com/deploy/nvml-api/group__nvmlDeviceQueries.html; verified 2026-07-11
claim: 'Retrieves total energy consumption for this GPU in millijoules (mJ) since the driver was last reloaded'; 'For Volta (tm) or newer fully supported devices'; returns unsigned long long; NVML_ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED if the device does not support energy readings

title: nvmlDeviceGetTotalEnergyConsumption availability + driver-reload/container semantics on actual Modal GPU SKUs
locator: n/a — requires a probe on the real rig
claim: Documented semantics (see nvml-energy-counter-semantics) do not guarantee per-SKU availability or well-defined reset behaviour inside Modal containers; P3-D-HW must probe each SKU before pinning the rig

title: From Words to Watts: Benchmarking the Energy Costs of Large Language Model Inference
venue: IEEE HPEC 2023 (venue verified: conference program listing + DOI 10.1109/HPEC58863.2023.10363447)
locator: arXiv abstract for content; ieee-hpec.org 2023 program + DOI for venue; venue upgraded from UNVERIFIED on 2026-07-11 re-check
claim: LLaMA-class inference energy benchmarking on V100 and A100, model sharding up to 32 GPUs, Alpaca + GSM8K datasets; precedent for LLM inference energy measurement shape, numbers are 7B+ and do not transfer to 100M-2B rungs

title: Scientific Benchmarking of Parallel Computing Systems
locator: primary PDF, rules 1-12 and the CI/quantile appendix material
claim: 12 rules verified at primary PDF: arithmetic mean for costs/harmonic for rates (R3), avoid summarizing ratios (R4), report CIs for nondeterministic data (R5), never assume normality without diagnostics (R6), compare via non-overlapping CIs/ANOVA (R7), percentiles for worst-case-latency problems (R8), document all varying factors (R9); rank-based distribution-free CIs for median/percentiles; CoV as consistency measure

title: A Nine Year Study of File System and Storage Benchmarking
locator: primary PDF re-read 2026-07-11: guidelines at Sec. 3.3-3.4 (pp. 5:7-5:8); survey compliance at Fig. 1 + Table I (pp. 5:9-5:10); authors' Auto-pilot config at p. 5:14
claim: 106 papers surveyed. Guidelines: disclose cache state; clear caches before EACH cold run (remount/purge/alloc-free); run each test several times with the NUMBER of runs determined from standard deviations or confidence levels (95% CIs recommended with half-width <5% of mean; Student's t below 30 runs) — NO fixed run count is prescribed; report full system state. 'At least ten times' was the authors' OWN Auto-pilot configuration, not a guideline. Survey found the majority of benchmarks did not specify run counts; per-conference MEDIAN run counts 1-3 (SOSP 1, FAST 1, OSDI 2, USENIX 3). CORRECTION 2026-07-11: an earlier revision of this entry misquoted a '>=10 runs' guideline and a 'median 7 runs' statistic (7 was a median count of reported system parameters, not runs)

title: wrk2 README — Coordinated Omission (G. Tene)
claim: Closed-loop load generators coordinate with the server to skip measuring high-latency periods; worked example: 1.4 s stall -> uncorrected p99 6.04 ms vs corrected 1.27 s (~200x); fix: constant-throughput schedule, latency measured from INTENDED send time. Scope note: this is a failure of closed-loop measurement presented as latency-under-load; closed-loop measurement of isolated service time (MLPerf SingleStream-style) is not invalidated

title: DistServe: Disaggregating Prefill and Decoding for Goodput-optimized Large Language Model Serving
claim: TTFT (prefill) and TPOT (decode) as separate SLOs; goodput = max per-GPU request rate meeting BOTH; 7.4x more requests or 12.6x tighter SLO vs colocated baselines at >90% SLO attainment; same model + hardware — a scheduling/accounting win, not a FLOP win

title: Producing Wrong Data Without Doing Anything Obviously Wrong!
claim: UNIX environment size and link order alone shift measured performance by >10% (double-digit % cases); remedies: randomize experimental setup across runs, use diverse workloads, causal analysis

title: The Five-minute Rule Thirty Years Later and its Impact on the Storage Hierarchy
claim: Break-even residency interval = (pages/$ RAM)/(accesses/s/$ device); 2017: DRAM-SATA-SSD @4KB ~7 min (15 min in 2007); DRAM-NVMe/3D-XPoint ~40 s; DRAM-HDD @4KB ratio 1,700->10,000, interval grew to hours, literal five-minute rule survives only at 512KB HDD pages. Scope note: break-evens are hardware- and price-generation-specific and do not cover cloud RAM billing, scale-to-zero, capacity constraints, access skew, or opportunity cost — residency-optimality conclusions for our stores are a hypothesis for KOT-LIFE/1, not licensed by this paper

title: SmolLM2: When Smol Goes Big — Data-Centric Training of a Small Language Model
claim: 1.7B explicitly 'overtrain[ed] on ~11 trillion tokens' (multi-stage mix) for strong small-model performance. Scope note: 'overtrained' is the direction Sardana prescribes under high inference demand but does NOT establish Sardana-optimality for any specified quality/workload/hardware/lifetime-demand/cost model; how much of the train-longer-deploy-smaller gain is banked is unquantified

title: SmolLM2-135M model card
claim: 135M trained on 2 trillion tokens (FineWeb-Edu, DCLM, The Stack + filtered sets), Apache-2.0, framed for on-device use; ~14,800 tokens/param — beyond Sardana's swept range (arithmetic ours)
